message:
feat(agent-sweep): add NTM driver and utility functions

Implement core NTM integration functions for agent-sweep:
- ntm_spawn_session(): spawn Claude Code sessions via robot mode
- ntm_send_prompt(): send prompts with automatic >4KB chunking
- ntm_wait_completion(): wait for agent idle state
- sanitize_session_name(): clean strings for tmux session names

Add agent-sweep utility functions:
- has_uncommitted_changes(): detect dirty repos
- repo_spec_to_path(): resolve owner/repo to filesystem path
- load_all_repos(): load repos from config files
- strip_ansi(): remove escape codes from pane output
- get_file_size_mb(): portable file size checking
- array_contains(), get_repo_name(): array and string helpers

Add result tracking functions:
- setup_agent_sweep_results(): initialize NDJSON tracking
- get_results_summary(): aggregate results with jq fallback
- mark_repo_completed(), is_repo_completed(): resume support
- filter_completed_repos(): filter for resume mode

Fix json_get_field() boolean false handling in jq fallback.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-0ac9","title":"Write parallel mode and rate limiting tests","description":"Implements detailed tests for parallel processing and work queue functionality.

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Test File
scripts/test_parallel_mode.sh

## Purpose
Verify parallel mode correctly distributes work across workers, handles locking, and aggregates results. Critical for reliability when processing many repos.

## Work Queue Tests

### Basic Distribution
- Test repos are distributed across workers
- Test each worker gets approximately equal work
- Test no repo is processed twice

### Lock Handling
- Test directory lock acquisition
- Test lock release on completion
- Test lock contention handling
- Test stale lock cleanup

## Rate Limit Backoff Tests

### Global Backoff
- Test backoff triggers when rate_limited detected
- Test exponential increase (1s -> 2s -> 4s -> 8s)
- Test jitter is applied (randomness)
- Test max backoff cap (300s)
- Test backoff reset after success

### Cross-Worker Coordination
- Test all workers honor global backoff
- Test backoff file is created/read correctly
- Test recovery after backoff period

## Result Aggregation Tests

### Success Aggregation
- Test per-worker results are collected
- Test summary counts are correct
- Test exit code reflects worst failure

### Partial Failure Handling
- Test one worker failure doesnt stop others
- Test failed repos are reported in summary
- Test state file records failures for resume

## Logging Requirements
- Each parallel operation logged with worker ID
- Lock acquisition/release logged
- Rate limit events logged with backoff duration
- Final summary shows per-worker stats

## Related Beads
- Tests: bd-2axv (run_parallel_agent_sweep)
- Tests: bd-7v3i (global rate limit backoff)
- Parent epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Work is distributed evenly across workers
- [ ] No race conditions in lock handling
- [ ] Rate limit backoff prevents API hammering
- [ ] All workers recover after backoff
- [ ] Results aggregated correctly from all workers
- [ ] Partial failures handled gracefully
- [ ] Detailed logging for each worker operation","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:26:29.517525498-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T17:26:29.517525498-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-0ac9","depends_on_id":"bd-2axv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:37.485261319-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-0ac9","depends_on_id":"bd-7v3i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:37.506751038-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-0ac9","depends_on_id":"bd-2ze9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:38:02.174028731-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-0ghe","title":"Implement secret scanning with layered fallback","description":"# Secret Scanning Implementation

## Parent Epic: bd-jk4n (Security Guardrails & Validation)

## Purpose
Block pushes if secrets detected in staged changes.

## Layered Approach

1. **If gitleaks installed**: Full scan (most comprehensive)
2. **Elif detect-secrets installed**: Alternative scanner
3. **Else**: Heuristic pattern matching (best effort)

## Implementation

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_SECRET_SCAN="${AGENT_SWEEP_SECRET_SCAN:-auto}"

run_secret_scan() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    SCAN_FINDINGS=""
    
    [[ "$AGENT_SWEEP_SECRET_SCAN" == "off" ]] && return 0
    
    # Prefer gitleaks
    if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then
        if ! SCAN_FINDINGS=$(gitleaks detect --source="$repo_path" --no-git 2>&1); then
            return 1  # Secrets found
        fi
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Fallback: detect-secrets
    if command -v detect-secrets &>/dev/null; then
        # Parse JSON output for findings
        ...
    fi
    
    # Last resort: heuristic patterns
    run_secret_scan_heuristic "$repo_path"
}
```

## Heuristic Patterns

```bash
patterns=(
    "-----BEGIN.*PRIVATE KEY-----"
    "AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}"           # AWS Access Key
    "ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}"        # GitHub PAT
    "sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{48}"         # OpenAI API Key
    "xox[baprs]-[0-9a-zA-Z]{10,}"  # Slack Token
    "password\\s*=\\s*[\"'][^\\s]{8,}"
)
```

## Integration

Secret scan runs AFTER denylist check, BEFORE commit execution.
If secrets found: block commit, write report, mark repo failed.

## Configuration

- --secret-scan=auto: Use available scanner (default)
- --secret-scan=on: Force heuristic if no scanner
- --secret-scan=off: Disable (not recommended)

## Output

SCAN_FINDINGS variable contains details for artifact report.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:53:26.873827681-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:53:26.873827681-05:00"}

 {"id":"bd-0jeu","title":"[EPIC] Documentation Updates","description":"# Documentation Updates

## Files to Update

### 1. README.md
Add new section for agent-sweep command:
- Purpose and benefits
- Prerequisites (ntm, tmux)
- Basic usage examples
- Options reference
- Exit codes
- Example output (normal and JSON)
- Troubleshooting

### 2. AGENTS.md
Add guidelines for agent-sweep:
- How AI agents should handle repos during sweep
- Commit message conventions
- Release workflow expectations
- What NOT to do (edit code, commit secrets, etc.)

### 3. Inline Help
show_agent_sweep_help() function:
- Brief description
- All options with defaults
- Examples
- Related commands

## README Additions

```markdown
### agent-sweep

Automated AI-assisted repository maintenance:

\`\`\`bash
# Basic sweep (commit only)
ru agent-sweep

# With release automation
ru agent-sweep --with-release

# Parallel processing
ru agent-sweep -j 4

# Dry run preview
ru agent-sweep --dry-run

# Resume interrupted sweep
ru agent-sweep --resume
\`\`\`

**Prerequisites:**
- ntm (installed automatically or via `curl -fsSL .../ntm/install.sh | bash`)
- tmux
- Claude Code

**Security:**
- Secret scanning before push
- File denylist enforcement
- Size limits on commits
```

## Configuration Reference
Add to Appendix C:
- AGENT_SWEEP_* environment variables
- Per-repo config format (.ru/agent-sweep.conf)
- User-level per-repo overrides

## Troubleshooting Section
- "ntm not installed" - Install command
- "tmux not available" - Platform-specific install
- "Session already exists" - Cleanup orphans
- "Rate limit detected" - Wait or reduce parallelism
- "Secrets detected" - Review blocked files","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:47:59.966410249-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:47:59.966410249-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-0jeu","depends_on_id":"bd-bx6s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:20.211752623-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-0jeu","depends_on_id":"bd-mkoc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:27.610219128-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-6kme","title":"Implement portable JSON parsing (json_get_field)","description":"# Portable JSON Parsing Implementation

## Parent Epic: bd-9o2h (NTM Driver Integration Layer)

## Purpose
Create json_get_field() function that works on systems without jq using layered fallbacks.

## Implementation

```bash
json_get_field() {
    local json="$1" field="$2"
    
    # Best: jq (most reliable)
    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        jq -r --arg f "$field" '.[$f] // empty' <<<"$json" 2>/dev/null
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Fallback: python3
    if command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then
        python3 -c "
import json,sys
field=sys.argv[1]
data=json.loads(sys.stdin.read())
v=data.get(field,'')
print(v if isinstance(v,(str,int,float,bool)) else json.dumps(v))
" "$field" <<<"$json" 2>/dev/null
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Fallback: perl with JSON::PP
    if command -v perl &>/dev/null && perl -MJSON::PP -e1 2>/dev/null; then
        perl -MJSON::PP -0777 -ne '...' "$field" <<<"$json" 2>/dev/null
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Last resort: minimal sed (flat strings only, fragile)
    sed -nE 's/.*"'"$field"'":[[:space:]]*"([^"]*)".*/\1/p' <<<"$json" | head -n1
}
```

## Also Implement
- json_is_success() - Check if JSON has success:true
- json_escape() - Escape string for JSON embedding (backslash, quotes, newlines, tabs)

## Testing
- Test with jq available
- Test with only python3
- Test with only perl
- Test with only sed (minimal fallback)
- Test nested objects (should return JSON for non-primitives)

## Considerations
- sed fallback is fragile, only works for simple flat strings
- Always try to use jq/python when available
- Document limitations in code comments","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:48:37.5200696-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:02:30.82913617-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:02:30.82913617-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented json_get_field(), json_is_success(), json_escape() with layered fallbacks (jq→python3→perl→sed). All 31 unit tests pass. Boolean false handling fixed in jq."}

-{"id":"bd-6nuc","title":"Implement per-repo configuration loading","description":"Implements load_repo_agent_config() to support per-repository agent-sweep customization.

## Background

The NTM integration plan specifies support for per-repo .ru-agent.yml or .ru-agent.json files that can override default agent-sweep behavior. This enables repository maintainers to:
- Enable/disable agent sweeps for their repo
- Customize file denylist patterns
- Set repo-specific size limits
- Configure custom phase prompts or skip certain phases
- Define pre/post hooks

## Function to Implement

### load_repo_agent_config()

Purpose: Load and merge per-repo configuration with global defaults.

Location: ~/.config/ru/agent-sweep.yml (global) merged with repo/.ru-agent.yml

Config Schema:
```yaml
# Enable/disable for this repo
enabled: true

# Override file patterns to deny
file_denylist_extra:
  - "*.generated.ts"
  - "vendor/*"

# Override file size limit (bytes)
max_file_size: 5242880  # 5MB

# Skip phases
skip_phases:
  - release  # Skip phase 3 for this repo

# Custom understanding context
extra_context: |
  This repo uses a monorepo structure.
  Database migrations are in db/migrations/.

# Hooks (run in repo context)
pre_sweep_hook: "./scripts/pre-agent.sh"
post_sweep_hook: "./scripts/post-agent.sh"
```

Implementation Steps:
1. Check for .ru-agent.yml or .ru-agent.json in repo root
2. Parse YAML/JSON using portable approach (yq -> python3 -> simple parser)
3. Merge with global config (repo overrides global)
4. Validate configuration values
5. Return merged config as associative array or temp file

## Portable YAML Parsing

Since this is pure Bash, implement layered fallback:
1. yq (if available) - fastest, most reliable
2. python3 -c "import yaml..." (common fallback)
3. Simple key: value parser for flat configs

## Error Handling

- Missing config file is NOT an error (use defaults)
- Invalid YAML/JSON should warn but not fail the sweep
- Unknown keys should warn but be ignored
- Validate types (numbers are numbers, booleans are booleans)

## Related Beads

- Parent epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)
- Used by: bd-kczb (cmd_agent_sweep main function)
- Used by: bd-b00c (run_single_agent_workflow)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Loads .ru-agent.yml from repo root if present
- [ ] Falls back to .ru-agent.json if YAML not found
- [ ] Merges repo config with global defaults correctly
- [ ] Unknown keys logged but do not cause failure
- [ ] Invalid config values produce clear warnings
- [ ] Works without yq installed (python3 fallback)
- [ ] Works without python3 installed (simple parser fallback)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:22:02.187186112-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T17:22:02.187186112-05:00"}

 {"id":"bd-6p3o","title":"Write comprehensive security guardrail tests","description":"Implements detailed tests for all security guardrails with extensive logging.

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Test File
scripts/test_security_guardrails.sh

## Purpose
Ensure security guardrails work correctly and cannot be bypassed. Every test must have detailed logging to verify exactly what was checked and why it passed/failed.

## Test Categories

### 1. File Denylist Tests

```bash
test_denylist_exact_matches() {
    log_test_start "Exact denylist matches"
    
    local denied_files=(
        ".env"
        ".env.local"
        ".env.production"
        "config/.env"
        "id_rsa"
        "id_ed25519"
        ".git/config"
        "node_modules/package/index.js"
        "secrets.json"
        "credentials.yaml"
    )
    
    for file in "${denied_files[@]}"; do
        log_verbose "Testing denylist for: $file"
        if \! is_file_denied "$file"; then
            log_error "FAIL: $file should be denied"
            return 1
        fi
        log_success "PASS: $file correctly denied"
    done
}

test_denylist_glob_patterns() {
    log_test_start "Glob pattern matching"
    
    # Test *.pem pattern
    is_file_denied "server.pem" || fail "*.pem should match server.pem"
    is_file_denied "certs/client.pem" || fail "*.pem should match nested"
    
    # Test **/node_modules/** pattern
    is_file_denied "frontend/node_modules/pkg/a.js" || fail "node_modules nested"
    
    # Test .git/** pattern
    is_file_denied ".git/objects/abc123" || fail ".git internals"
}

test_denylist_allowed_files() {
    log_test_start "Allowed file verification"
    
    local allowed_files=(
        "src/main.py"
        "README.md"
        "config/settings.yaml"  # Not secrets.yaml
        ".gitignore"
        "package.json"
    )
    
    for file in "${allowed_files[@]}"; do
        log_verbose "Testing allow for: $file"
        if is_file_denied "$file"; then
            log_error "FAIL: $file should be allowed but was denied"
            return 1
        fi
        log_success "PASS: $file correctly allowed"
    done
}
```

### 2. File Size Limit Tests

```bash
test_file_size_enforcement() {
    log_test_start "File size limit enforcement"
    
    local test_dir=$(mktemp -d)
    
    # Create files of various sizes
    dd if=/dev/zero of="$test_dir/small.bin" bs=1K count=100 2>/dev/null
    dd if=/dev/zero of="$test_dir/at_limit.bin" bs=1M count=10 2>/dev/null
    dd if=/dev/zero of="$test_dir/over_limit.bin" bs=1M count=11 2>/dev/null
    
    log_verbose "Testing 100KB file (should pass)"
    is_file_oversized "$test_dir/small.bin" 10485760 && fail "100KB should pass"
    
    log_verbose "Testing 10MB file at limit (should pass)"
    is_file_oversized "$test_dir/at_limit.bin" 10485760 && fail "At limit should pass"
    
    log_verbose "Testing 11MB file over limit (should fail)"
    is_file_oversized "$test_dir/over_limit.bin" 10485760 || fail "Over limit should fail"
    
    rm -rf "$test_dir"
    log_success "All size tests passed"
}
```

### 3. Binary Detection Tests

```bash
test_binary_detection() {
    log_test_start "Binary file detection"
    
    local test_dir=$(mktemp -d)
    
    # Create text file
    echo "Hello, world" > "$test_dir/text.txt"
    
    # Create binary file (has null bytes)
    printf "binary\x00content" > "$test_dir/binary.bin"
    
    # Create ELF header
    printf "\x7fELF" > "$test_dir/executable"
    
    log_verbose "Testing text file (should not be binary)"
    is_binary_file "$test_dir/text.txt" && fail "Text should not be binary"
    
    log_verbose "Testing binary file (should be binary)"
    is_binary_file "$test_dir/binary.bin" || fail "Null bytes should be binary"
    
    log_verbose "Testing ELF file (should be binary)"
    is_binary_file "$test_dir/executable" || fail "ELF should be binary"
    
    rm -rf "$test_dir"
    log_success "All binary detection tests passed"
}
```

### 4. Secret Scanning Tests

```bash
test_secret_scanning_gitleaks() {
    log_test_start "Secret scanning with gitleaks"
    
    # Skip if gitleaks not available
    if \! command -v gitleaks >/dev/null; then
        log_skip "gitleaks not installed"
        return 0
    fi
    
    local test_dir=$(mktemp -d)
    git init "$test_dir" >/dev/null 2>&1
    
    # Create file with fake AWS key
    echo "AWS_SECRET_KEY=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE" > "$test_dir/config.sh"
    
    log_verbose "Scanning for secrets in config.sh"
    if scan_for_secrets "$test_dir/config.sh"; then
        log_error "FAIL: Should detect AWS key pattern"
        return 1
    fi
    
    rm -rf "$test_dir"
    log_success "gitleaks correctly detected secret"
}

test_secret_scanning_fallback() {
    log_test_start "Secret scanning with heuristic fallback"
    
    local test_dir=$(mktemp -d)
    
    # Test patterns that should trigger heuristic detection
    local secret_patterns=(
        "password=supersecret123"
        "api_key: sk-abc123def456"
        "PRIVATE_KEY-----BEGIN RSA"
        "Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGc..."
    )
    
    for pattern in "${secret_patterns[@]}"; do
        echo "$pattern" > "$test_dir/test.txt"
        log_verbose "Testing heuristic for: ${pattern:0:30}..."
        
        if \! heuristic_secret_scan "$test_dir/test.txt"; then
            # Expected to detect
            log_success "Correctly detected: ${pattern:0:30}..."
        else
            log_error "FAIL: Should detect: ${pattern:0:30}..."
            rm -rf "$test_dir"
            return 1
        fi
    done
    
    rm -rf "$test_dir"
}
```

## Logging Requirements

Every test MUST:
1. Call log_test_start() with test name
2. Use log_verbose() for each check
3. Use log_success() or log_error() for results
4. Report total assertions passed/failed

 {"id":"bd-9s7y","title":"Implement local driver (tmux + stream-json)","description":"# Task: Implement Local Driver (tmux + stream-json)

## Purpose
Implement the session driver interface using tmux and Claude's stream-json output mode. This is the fallback driver when ntm is not available.

## Background
Claude Code supports --output-format stream-json which emits NDJSON events:
- System init with session_id and available tools
- Assistant messages with text and tool_use
- Tool results
- Final result with status

We capture this output and parse it for question detection.

## Implementation

### local_driver_start_session()
```bash
local_driver_start_session() {
    local wt_path="$1"
    local session_name="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    
    local log_file="$wt_path/.ru/session.log"
    local event_pipe="$wt_path/.ru/events.pipe"
    
    # Create named pipe for event streaming
    rm -f "$event_pipe"
    mkfifo "$event_pipe"
    
    # Build claude command
    local claude_cmd="claude -p $(printf '%q' "$prompt") --output-format stream-json"
    
    # Create tmux session
    tmux new-session -d -s "$session_name" -c "$wt_path" \
        "exec $claude_cmd 2>&1 | tee '$log_file' > '$event_pipe'"
    
    # Return session info
    cat << EOF
{
  "session_id": "$session_name",
  "worktree": "$wt_path",
  "log_file": "$log_file",
  "event_pipe": "$event_pipe",
  "started_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
}
EOF
}
```

### local_driver_send_to_session()
```bash
local_driver_send_to_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
    
    # Send via tmux
    tmux send-keys -t "$session_id" "$message" Enter
    
    return $?
}
```

### local_driver_get_session_state()
```bash
local_driver_get_session_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Check if tmux session exists
    if ! tmux has-session -t "$session_id" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo '{"state": "complete", "reason": "session_ended"}'
        return
    fi
    
    # Capture last lines of output
    local output
    output=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$session_id" -p -S -50 2>/dev/null)
    
    # Detect state from output
    local state="generating"
    local wait_reason=""
    
    # Check for prompt (waiting)
    if echo "$output" | grep -qE '^\s*>\s*$|Press Enter|Select.*:|Enter.*:'; then
        state="waiting"
        wait_reason="prompt_detected"
    fi
    
    # Check for errors
    if echo "$output" | grep -qiE 'error:|panic:|rate.limit|429'; then
        state="error"
    fi
    
    cat << EOF
{
  "state": "$state",
  "wait_reason": "$wait_reason",
  "session_id": "$session_id"
}
EOF
}
```

### local_driver_stream_events()
```bash
local_driver_stream_events() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    local wt_path="$3"
    
    local event_pipe="$wt_path/.ru/events.pipe"
    local log_file="$wt_path/.ru/session.log"
    
    # Parse stream-json events
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
        
        # Parse JSON event type
        local event_type
        event_type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // empty' 2>/dev/null)
        
        case "$event_type" in
            system)
                local session_id_from_event
                session_id_from_event=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.session_id // empty')
                $callback "init" "$session_id_from_event"
                ;;
            assistant)
                # Check for AskUserQuestion tool_use
                local tool_name
                tool_name=$(echo "$line" | jq -r \
                    '.message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use") | .name // empty' 2>/dev/null)
                
                if [[ "$tool_name" == "AskUserQuestion" ]]; then
                    local question_data
                    question_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c \
                        '.message.content[] | select(.name=="AskUserQuestion") | .input')
                    $callback "question" "$question_data"
                fi
                ;;
            result)
                local status
                status=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.status // "unknown"')
                $callback "complete" "$status"
                break
                ;;
        esac
    done < "$event_pipe"
}
```

### local_driver_stop_session()
```bash
local_driver_stop_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Kill tmux session
    tmux kill-session -t "$session_id" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    return 0
}
```

### local_driver_interrupt_session()
```bash
local_driver_interrupt_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Send Ctrl+C
    tmux send-keys -t "$session_id" C-c
    
    return 0
}
```

## Session Monitoring Loop
```bash
local_driver_monitor_sessions() {
    local -a sessions=("$@")
    
    while [[ ${#sessions[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do
        local -a active_sessions=()
        
        for session_id in "${sessions[@]}"; do
            local state
            state=$(local_driver_get_session_state "$session_id" | jq -r '.state')
            
            case "$state" in
                complete)
                    handle_session_complete "$session_id"
                    ;;
                error)
                    handle_session_error "$session_id"
                    ;;
                waiting)
                    handle_session_waiting "$session_id"
                    active_sessions+=("$session_id")
                    ;;
                *)
                    active_sessions+=("$session_id")
                    ;;
            esac
        done
        
        sessions=("${active_sessions[@]}")
        sleep 2
    done
}
```

## Testing
- Launch session, verify tmux created
- Send message, verify delivered
- Detect AskUserQuestion event
- Detect session completion
- Interrupt and stop work correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sessions launch in tmux with stream-json
- [ ] Events parsed from NDJSON stream
- [ ] AskUserQuestion detected
- [ ] Messages sent via tmux send-keys
- [ ] Session cleanup works
- [ ] Works without ntm installed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:35:14.53945729-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:46.375298473-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:46.375298473-05:00","close_reason":"Local driver implemented with: local_driver_start_session (tmux + stream-json), local_driver_send_to_session, local_driver_get_session_state, local_driver_stop_session, local_driver_interrupt_session, local_driver_stream_events (stub for bd-8zt6), local_driver_list_sessions, local_driver_session_alive. Driver routing via _enable_local_driver().  {"id":"bd-9xjc","title":"Phase 6: Error Handling and Recovery","description":"Phase 6: Error Handling and Recovery

Overview
--------
Robust error handling, retry strategies, checkpointing, and recovery
mechanisms to handle the many failure modes in a multi-session system.

Why Critical?
-------------
Many things can fail:
- Network timeouts
- API rate limits (GitHub, Anthropic)
- Session crashes
- User interrupts (Ctrl+C)
- Disk full
- Authentication expiry

Without proper handling, partial work is lost.

Components
----------

6.1 Error Categories
   Classify errors for appropriate handling:
   - Transient: retry with backoff
   - Rate Limit: backoff + rotate
   - Auth: alert user, pause
   - Session: restart, preserve state
   - User: graceful handling
   - Fatal: abort with clear message

6.2 Retry with Exponential Backoff
   For transient failures:
   - Base delay with exponential growth
   - Jitter to prevent thundering herd
   - Max attempts limit
   - Configurable per operation

6.3 Checkpointing
   Periodic state saves for recovery:
   - After each significant operation
   - Includes all necessary context
   - Atomic writes prevent corruption
   - Configurable interval

6.4 Resume from Checkpoint
   Continue interrupted reviews:
   - Load checkpoint state
   - Identify completed/pending work
   - Skip already-done repos
   - Restore question queue

6.5 Graceful Degradation
   When advanced features unavailable:
   - ntm missing: use local driver
   - gum missing: use ANSI fallback
   - Tests fail: continue without push
   - Rate limited: reduce parallelism

6.6 Signal Handling
   Proper cleanup on interrupts:
   - SIGINT (Ctrl+C): save state, exit
   - SIGTERM: save state, exit
   - SIGHUP: continue in background?
   - Set SYNC_INTERRUPTED flag

Exit Criteria
-------------
- All error categories handled
- Retry logic works correctly
- Checkpoints saved periodically
- Resume continues from checkpoint
- Graceful degradation works
- Signals handled cleanly

Estimated Effort
----------------
~300 lines of Bash","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:59.930455089-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:29:14.566719244-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T21:29:14.566719244-05:00","close_reason":"All Phase 6 components verified as implemented: error categories (classify_review_error), retry with exponential backoff (retry_with_backoff), checkpointing (checkpoint_review_state/load_review_checkpoint), resume functionality (--resume flag in cmd_review), signal handling (trap for INT/TERM), and graceful degradation (GUM_AVAILABLE, detect_review_driver fallbacks)"}
-{"id":"bd-a15t","title":"Implement result tracking and aggregation","description":"Implements result tracking functions for agent-sweep execution.

## Parent Epic: bd-1vfe (State Management & Artifacts)

## Purpose
Track per-repo results during sweep execution for summary reporting and resume capability.

## Functions to Implement

### setup_agent_sweep_results()
```bash
# Initialize results tracking
setup_agent_sweep_results() {
    RESULTS_FILE="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/results.ndjson"
    RUN_ID="$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)-$$"
    RUN_START_TIME=$(date +%s)

    # Create run directory for artifacts
    RUN_ARTIFACTS_DIR="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/runs/${RUN_ID}"
    mkdir -p "$RUN_ARTIFACTS_DIR"

    # Initialize results file with header
    echo "{\"run_id\":\"$RUN_ID\",\"started_at\":\"$(date -Iseconds)\",\"type\":\"header\"}" > "$RESULTS_FILE"

    # Track counts
    SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT=0
    SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT=0
    SWEEP_SKIP_COUNT=0
}
```

### write_result()
```bash
# Write a single repo result
# Args: $1=repo_name, $2=operation, $3=status, $4=duration_ms, $5=details, $6=repo_path
write_result() {
    local repo_name="$1"
    local operation="$2"
    local status="$3"
    local duration_ms="$4"
    local details="${5:-}"
    local repo_path="${6:-}"
    local timestamp=$(date -Iseconds)

    # Build JSON (use jq if available, else manual)
    local result_json
    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        result_json=$(jq -nc \
            --arg repo "$repo_name" \
            --arg op "$operation" \
            --arg status "$status" \
            --arg ts "$timestamp" \
            --argjson dur "$duration_ms" \
            --arg details "$details" \
            --arg path "$repo_path" \
            '{repo:$repo, operation:$op, status:$status, timestamp:$ts, duration_ms:$dur, details:$details, path:$path, type:"result"}')
    else
        result_json="{\"repo\":\"$repo_name\",\"operation\":\"$operation\",\"status\":\"$status\",\"timestamp\":\"$timestamp\",\"duration_ms\":$duration_ms,\"type\":\"result\"}"
    fi

    # Atomic append (lock in parallel mode)
    if [[ "${AGENT_SWEEP_PARALLEL:-1}" -gt 1 ]]; then
        dir_lock_acquire "${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/locks/results.lock" 30
        echo "$result_json" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
        dir_lock_release "${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/locks/results.lock"
    else
        echo "$result_json" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
    fi

    # Update counts
    case "$status" in
        success) ((SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT++)) ;;
        failed|error) ((SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT++)) ;;
        skipped|preflight_failed) ((SWEEP_SKIP_COUNT++)) ;;
    esac
}
```

### get_results_summary()
```bash
# Aggregate results for summary
get_results_summary() {
    local results_file="${1:-$RESULTS_FILE}"

    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        jq -s '
            [.[] | select(.type == "result")] |
            {
                total: length,
                succeeded: [.[] | select(.status == "success")] | length,
                failed: [.[] | select(.status | IN("failed", "error"))] | length,
                skipped: [.[] | select(.status | IN("skipped", "preflight_failed"))] | length,
                repos: [.[] | {repo, status, duration_ms}]
            }
        ' < "$results_file"
    else
        # Fallback: just return counts from variables
        echo "{\"total\":$((SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT + SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT + SWEEP_SKIP_COUNT)),\"succeeded\":$SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT,\"failed\":$SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT,\"skipped\":$SWEEP_SKIP_COUNT}"
    fi
}
```

### mark_repo_completed()
```bash
# Mark repo as completed for resume tracking
mark_repo_completed() {
    local repo_spec="$1"
    COMPLETED_REPOS+=("$repo_spec")
}
```

## Results File Format (NDJSON)
```
{"run_id":"20260106-153000-12345","started_at":"2026-01-06T15:30:00Z","type":"header"}
{"repo":"owner/repo1","operation":"agent-sweep","status":"success","timestamp":"2026-01-06T15:31:00Z","duration_ms":45000,"type":"result"}
{"repo":"owner/repo2","operation":"agent-sweep","status":"failed","timestamp":"2026-01-06T15:32:00Z","duration_ms":30000,"details":"timeout","type":"result"}
```

- Called by: setup_agent_sweep_results() from cmd_agent_sweep()
- Called by: write_result() from run_single_agent_workflow() and run_sequential/parallel_sweep()
- Used by: print_agent_sweep_summary()
- Used by: state file management for resume

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Results file created with unique run ID
- [ ] Each repo writes result after completion
- [ ] Parallel mode uses locking for atomic writes
- [ ] Summary aggregation works with jq and without
- [ ] Duration tracked in milliseconds
- [ ] Details field captures failure reasons","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:37:12.21239494-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T17:37:12.21239494-05:00"}

```bash
# Write a single repo result
# Args: $1=repo_name, $2=operation, $3=status, $4=duration_ms, $5=details, $6=repo_path
write_result() {
    local repo_name="$1"
    local operation="$2"
    local status="$3"
    local duration_ms="$4"
    local details="${5:-}"
    local repo_path="${6:-}"
    local timestamp=$(date -Iseconds)

    # Build JSON (use jq if available, else manual)
    local result_json
    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        result_json=$(jq -nc \
            --arg repo "$repo_name" \
            --arg op "$operation" \
            --arg status "$status" \
            --arg ts "$timestamp" \
            --argjson dur "$duration_ms" \
            --arg details "$details" \
            --arg path "$repo_path" \
            '{repo:$repo, operation:$op, status:$status, timestamp:$ts, duration_ms:$dur, details:$details, path:$path, type:"result"}')
    else
        result_json="{\"repo\":\"$repo_name\",\"operation\":\"$operation\",\"status\":\"$status\",\"timestamp\":\"$timestamp\",\"duration_ms\":$duration_ms,\"type\":\"result\"}"
    fi

    # Atomic append (lock in parallel mode)
    if [[ "${AGENT_SWEEP_PARALLEL:-1}" -gt 1 ]]; then
        dir_lock_acquire "${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/locks/results.lock" 30
        echo "$result_json" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
        dir_lock_release "${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/locks/results.lock"
    else
        echo "$result_json" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
    fi

    # Update counts
    case "$status" in
        success) ((SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT++)) ;;
        failed|error) ((SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT++)) ;;
        skipped|preflight_failed) ((SWEEP_SKIP_COUNT++)) ;;
    esac
}
```

### get_results_summary()
```bash
# Aggregate results for summary
get_results_summary() {
    local results_file="${1:-$RESULTS_FILE}"

    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        jq -s '
            [.[] | select(.type == "result")] |
            {
                total: length,
                succeeded: [.[] | select(.status == "success")] | length,
                failed: [.[] | select(.status | IN("failed", "error"))] | length,
                skipped: [.[] | select(.status | IN("skipped", "preflight_failed"))] | length,
                repos: [.[] | {repo, status, duration_ms}]
            }
        ' < "$results_file"
    else
        # Fallback: just return counts from variables
        echo "{\"total\":$((SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT + SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT + SWEEP_SKIP_COUNT)),\"succeeded\":$SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT,\"failed\":$SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT,\"skipped\":$SWEEP_SKIP_COUNT}"
    fi
}
```

### mark_repo_completed()
```bash
# Mark repo as completed for resume tracking
mark_repo_completed() {
    local repo_spec="$1"
    COMPLETED_REPOS+=("$repo_spec")
}
```

## Results File Format (NDJSON)
```
{"run_id":"20260106-153000-12345","started_at":"2026-01-06T15:30:00Z","type":"header"}
{"repo":"owner/repo1","operation":"agent-sweep","status":"success","timestamp":"2026-01-06T15:31:00Z","duration_ms":45000,"type":"result"}
{"repo":"owner/repo2","operation":"agent-sweep","status":"failed","timestamp":"2026-01-06T15:32:00Z","duration_ms":30000,"details":"timeout","type":"result"}
```

## Integration Points
- Called by: setup_agent_sweep_results() from cmd_agent_sweep()
- Called by: write_result() from run_single_agent_workflow() and run_sequential/parallel_sweep()
- Used by: print_agent_sweep_summary()
- Used by: state file management for resume

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Results file created with unique run ID
- [ ] Each repo writes result after completion
- [ ] Parallel mode uses locking for atomic writes
- [ ] Summary aggregation works with jq and without
- [ ] Duration tracked in milliseconds
- [ ] Details field captures failure reasons","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:37:12.21239494-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:11:53.452754108-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:11:53.452754108-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented setup_agent_sweep_results(), get_results_summary(), mark_repo_completed(), is_repo_completed(), filter_completed_repos(). ShellCheck clean."}

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests (test_unit_ntm_driver.sh)
- ntm_check_available_not_installed
- ntm_check_available_functional
- json_get_field (with jq)
- json_get_field (without jq - fallbacks)
- json_is_success
- json_escape
- has_uncommitted_changes (clean and dirty repos)
- is_file_denied (denylist patterns)
- is_file_too_large
- is_binary_file
- repo_preflight_check (all skip reasons)

### 2. E2E Tests (test_e2e_agent_sweep.sh)
Using ntm mock that simulates scenarios:
- test_agent_sweep_dry_run
- test_agent_sweep_single_repo
- test_agent_sweep_multiple_repos
- test_agent_sweep_parallel
- test_agent_sweep_resume
- test_agent_sweep_with_release

### 3. Failure Mode Tests (NEW)
- test_agent_sweep_timeout
- test_agent_sweep_resource_busy
- test_agent_sweep_agent_error
- test_agent_sweep_rate_limited
- test_agent_sweep_spawn_fail

### 4. Preflight Tests
- test_preflight_rebase_in_progress
- test_preflight_merge_in_progress
- test_preflight_detached_head
- test_preflight_diverged
- test_preflight_too_many_untracked

### 5. Security Tests
- test_security_denylist
- test_security_file_size
- test_security_binary_detection
- test_secret_scan_heuristic

### 6. JSON Parsing Portability Tests
- test_json_get_field_with_jq
- test_json_get_field_with_python
- test_json_get_field_with_perl
- test_json_get_field_without_all (sed fallback)

### 7. Contract Fixtures
Store expected ntm responses in scripts/fixtures/ntm_responses.json:
- spawn_success, spawn_resource_busy
- wait_success, wait_timeout
- activity states

## ntm Mock Script
Creates fake ntm in test PATH that responds based on NTM_MOCK_SCENARIO:
- ok: Happy path
- timeout: Wait timeout
- resource_busy: Session exists
- agent_error: Agent crashes
- rate_limited: Rate limit detected
- spawn_fail: Spawn fails

## Test Utilities
Reuse from existing test framework:
- setup_test_env() / cleanup_test_env()
- assert_equals, assert_contains, assert_exit_code
- skip_test() for missing deps (tmux, jq)
- setup_dirty_repo() helper

## CI Integration
- Skip E2E tests if tmux not available
- Run all unit tests (no external deps)

+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# AGENT-SWEEP RESULT TRACKING
+# Functions for tracking per-repo results during sweep execution.
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+# Global result tracking state (initialized by setup_agent_sweep_results)
+declare -g AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR=""
+declare -g RUN_ID=""
+declare -g RUN_START_TIME=""
+declare -g RUN_ARTIFACTS_DIR=""
+declare -g SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT=0
+declare -g SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT=0
+declare -g SWEEP_SKIP_COUNT=0
+declare -ga COMPLETED_REPOS=()
+
+# Initialize agent-sweep results tracking
+# Sets up state directory, run ID, and results file
+setup_agent_sweep_results() {
+    local state_base="${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}/ru"
+    AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR="${state_base}/agent-sweep"
+    ensure_dir "$AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR"
+    ensure_dir "${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/locks"
+
+    RUN_ID="$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)-$$"
+    export RUN_START_TIME
+    RUN_START_TIME=$(date +%s)
+
+    # Create run directory for artifacts
+    RUN_ARTIFACTS_DIR="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/runs/${RUN_ID}"
+    ensure_dir "$RUN_ARTIFACTS_DIR"
+
+    # Set up results file (used by existing write_result function)
+    export RESULTS_FILE="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/results.ndjson"
+    export RESULTS_LOCK_DIR="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/locks/results.lock"
+
+    # Write header record
+    local header_ts
+    header_ts=$(date -Iseconds 2>/dev/null || date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
+    echo "{\"run_id\":\"$RUN_ID\",\"started_at\":\"$header_ts\",\"type\":\"header\"}" > "$RESULTS_FILE"
+
+    # Reset counts
+    SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT=0
+    SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT=0
+    SWEEP_SKIP_COUNT=0
+    COMPLETED_REPOS=()
+}
+
+# Aggregate results from NDJSON file for summary reporting
+# Usage: get_results_summary [results_file]
+# Outputs: JSON summary to stdout
+get_results_summary() {
+    local results_file="${1:-${RESULTS_FILE:-}}"
+    [[ -z "$results_file" || ! -f "$results_file" ]] && {
+        echo '{"total":0,"succeeded":0,"failed":0,"skipped":0}'
+        return
+    }
+
+    if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
+        jq -s '
+            [.[] | select(.type == "result" or .type == null)] |
+            {
+                total: length,
+                succeeded: [.[] | select(.status == "success")] | length,
+                failed: [.[] | select(.status | . == "failed" or . == "error")] | length,
+                skipped: [.[] | select(.status | . == "skipped" or . == "preflight")] | length,
+                repos: [.[] | {repo, status, duration}]
+            }
+        ' < "$results_file" 2>/dev/null || echo '{"total":0,"succeeded":0,"failed":0,"skipped":0}'
+    else
+        # Fallback: use tracked counts
+        local total=$((SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT + SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT + SWEEP_SKIP_COUNT))
+        echo "{\"total\":$total,\"succeeded\":$SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT,\"failed\":$SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT,\"skipped\":$SWEEP_SKIP_COUNT}"
+    fi
+}
+
+# Mark a repo as completed for resume tracking
+# Usage: mark_repo_completed repo_spec
+mark_repo_completed() {
+    local repo_spec="${1:-}"
+    [[ -z "$repo_spec" ]] && return 1
+    COMPLETED_REPOS+=("$repo_spec")
+
+    # Update status based on most recent operation
+    case "${2:-success}" in
+        success) ((SWEEP_SUCCESS_COUNT++)) ;;
+        failed|error) ((SWEEP_FAIL_COUNT++)) ;;
+        skipped|preflight) ((SWEEP_SKIP_COUNT++)) ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+# Check if a repo was already completed (for resume)
+# Usage: is_repo_completed repo_spec
+is_repo_completed() {
+    local repo_spec="${1:-}"
+    array_contains COMPLETED_REPOS "$repo_spec"
+}
+
+# Filter out completed repos from an array (for resume)
+# Usage: filter_completed_repos array_name
+filter_completed_repos() {
+    local -n arr_ref=$1
+    local filtered=()
+    local repo
+    for repo in "${arr_ref[@]}"; do
+        is_repo_completed "$repo" || filtered+=("$repo")
+    done
+    arr_ref=("${filtered[@]}")
+}
+

 mktemp_file() {
     local tmp
